messages.content:
CVE-2021-45059 is a use-after-free vulnerability identified in Adobe InDesign versions 16.4 and earlier. This vulnerability arises during the processing of JPEG2000 files, potentially allowing an attacker to disclose sensitive memory contents. The vulnerability is classified under CWE-416, characterized by referencing memory after it has been freed, which can lead to unexpected behavior or execution of code.

The severity of this vulnerability is assessed as LOW due to the requirement of user interaction; exploitation occurs when a victim opens a specially crafted malicious file. Successful exploitation may enable attackers to bypass security mitigations like Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR).

Affected software includes Adobe InDesign up to version 16.4. Mitigation techniques to consider include employing strategies such as user training to avoid opening untrusted files (M1017) and implementing security updates and patch management (M1038). Additionally, users can monitor system activity for abnormal behavior as outlined in MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1204. Providing regular software updates and maintaining a rigorous patch management routine are vital practices to minimize risks associated with this vulnerability.
